name: problem-solving-methodology
description: Step-by-step thinking process and problem-solving methodology used by the AI agent when debugging, fixing, and building features.

phases.name: Understand The Problem
steps: Read the user's description carefully - what they SEE vs what they EXPECT
Identify the specific error messages, logs, or symptoms mentioned
Determine if this is a NEW issue or a REGRESSION (worked before, broke now)
Check what SYSTEM components are involved (frontend, backend, API, database, Playwright)
Ask clarifying questions ONLY when critical information is missing
rules: Never assume - always verify by reading actual files and logs
Error messages are clues, not always the root cause
Distinguish between symptoms (what's visible) and root causes (what's broken)

phases.name: Investigate Evidence
steps: Read the ACTUAL files mentioned in errors (source code, config, logs)
Search the codebase for relevant patterns (grep for error text, function names, class names)
Check if similar issues were solved before (search archives, history, documentation)
Run diagnostic commands to verify the current state (list files, check logs, run test commands)
Compare working vs broken versions (what changed between them?)
rules: File system truth > code comments > documentation > assumptions
Always read files before making changes to them
If something worked before, find what was different then vs now
Archives and history contain proven solutions - read them first

phases.name: Root Cause Analysis
steps: Trace the data/control flow from entry point to failure point
For each step in the chain, ask: 'Could this be the source?'
Eliminate possibilities systematically until only one remains
Verify the root cause by: 'If I fix X, would the symptom disappear?'
Document the chain: Entry → Step A → Step B → FAILURE (root cause: X)

common_root_causes.windows_paths: Paths with spaces break spawn/exec without proper quoting
common_root_causes.angular_spa: Angular apps never reach networkidle due to background polling
common_root_causes.log_duplication: Multiple code paths emitting same event (double-emit bug)
common_root_causes.form_validation: Angular reactive forms disable submit button when invalid
common_root_causes.kendo_components: Inner inputs don't have formcontrolname - it's on the wrapper
common_root_causes.modal_vs_inline: Forms can be modal dialogs OR inline panels - selectors differ
common_root_causes.cache_state: Old session/cache data makes system think it's resuming vs fresh start

phases.name: Design The Fix
steps: Start with the MINIMAL change needed (change least code possible)
Check if the fix could break anything else (read surrounding code)
Prevent the class of bug, not just this instance (fix the pattern)
If a working solution existed before, adapt it instead of inventing new
Plan the fix in layers: Immediate fix → Pattern fix → Prevention
principles: Minimal change principle: Change the least code needed to fix the issue
Pattern fix principle: If the same bug can happen elsewhere, fix the pattern
Prevention principle: Add guards/validation to prevent recurrence
Reuse principle: Use existing code patterns, don't reinvent

phases.name: Implement The Fix
steps: Make the code change (edit/add/delete files)
Run TypeScript compiler to verify no syntax/type errors
Run the specific test or scenario that was failing
If still failing, go back to Phase 2 (investigate) with new data
If passing, test edge cases and related functionality
rules: Always run TypeScript compiler before testing - catch errors early
Test the specific failure case first before testing broader functionality
If the fix doesn't work, don't add more fixes - find the REAL root cause
Document what was changed and WHY in comments or commit messages

phases.name: Verify And Document
steps: Run the full test suite to ensure no regressions
Verify the fix in the actual environment (not just unit tests)
Document the root cause and fix for future reference
Identify if other code has the same pattern that needs fixing
Update skill files or knowledge base with new learnings
deliverables: Clear summary of: What was broken, Why it broke, How it was fixed
Any remaining concerns or follow-up items
Updated skill files if new patterns were discovered

playwright_fails: Is it a path issue? Check for spaces in paths → use node.exe + cli.js
Is it a timeout? Angular SPA → use commit instead of networkidle
Is it a selector issue? Read actual HTML → find working selectors from harvester recordings
Is it a state issue? Check if previous test left dialogs/modals open
Is it an auth issue? Verify cookies/session are shared between tests
test_generation_fails: Check skill JSON file is valid (not corrupted, has required fields)
Check selectors use proper quote escaping (single quotes in selectors need escaping)
Check field names don't conflict (unique variable names for each field)
Check form actions are defined (buttonSelector, submitSelector must exist)
Check route/URL is correct (match what harvester recorded)
log_duplication: Search for all places that emit the same event
Check if callback ALSO emits (double-emit bug)
Check if console.log is intercepted AND original is called
Fix: Keep single source of truth, remove redundant emits
api_returns_401: Check if authentication cookie/token is being passed
Check if API URL matches the environment (test vs uat vs live)
Check if the API requires different auth than the frontend
Use page.evaluate() to call API from browser context (has cookies)

knowledge_sources.harvester_recordings: D:\KMT\My class\AI\GoHybridAI\_archive\Manual Recordings\ - Contains actual working selectors from real user sessions
knowledge_sources.skill_files: D:\KMT\My class\AI\GoHybridAI\backend\skills\GlobalHR\forms\ - Field selectors, test data, business rules per module
knowledge_sources.master_data_rules: D:\KMT\My class\AI\GoHybridAI\backend\skills\master-data-rules.json - Universal rules for all Master Data entities
knowledge_sources.navigation_patterns: D:\KMT\My class\AI\GoHybridAI\backend\skills\GlobalHR\navigation-patterns.json - Common action selectors (Add New, Save, OK, etc.)
knowledge_sources.archive_conversations: D:\KMT\My class\AI\GoHybridAI\_archive\ - Historical solutions, fixes, and debugging sessions

decision_tree.user_reports_bug: → Phase 1: Understand → Phase 2: Investigate → Phase 3: Root Cause → Phase 4: Design Fix → Phase 5: Implement → Phase 6: Verify
decision_tree.user_requests_feature: → Check skill files for existing knowledge → Use generic patterns if no specific knowledge → Build incrementally → Test each step
decision_tree.user_asks_why_something_fails: → Read actual error logs → Trace execution flow → Find first failure point → Explain the chain
decision_tree.test_execution_fails: → Check Playwright path quoting → Check navigation wait strategy → Check form selectors from harvester → Check API responses
decision_tree.test_generation_fails: → Check skill file is valid JSON → Check selectors have proper escaping → Check field types are supported → Check form actions exist

anti_patterns: Never add more code to work around a bug - fix the root cause
Never assume a file exists or has certain content - read it first
Never change multiple things at once - isolate the change
Never skip verification - always run the test/compiler after changes
Never ignore historical data - archives contain proven solutions
Never create duplicate functionality - reuse existing services/functions
Never leave debug logging in production code - clean up after debugging
source: Derived from actual problem-solving sessions across ATT-13, ATT-16, and system debugging (March-April 2026)
